sections.paragraph:
in pause בָּצַע, with suff. בִּצְעֵךְ m.

(1) rapine, prey (see the root No. 2), prop. of enemies, Jud. 5:19 Jer 51:13 Mic 4:13, also applied to the rapine of kings and nobles who despoil a people, Jer 22:17 Eze 22:13, and hence-

(2) to any unjust gain whatever, whether acquired from bribes ( 1Sa 8:3 Isa 33:15), or by other frauds ( Isa 57:17); Exo 18:21 Pro 28:16 and even-

(3) any gain, Isa 56:11 Eze 33:31 מַה־בֶּצַע “what profit is it?” Gen 37:26 Job 22:3 Psa 30:10.
